title: WOS Advanced Governance Document
description: A WOS Advanced Governance Document per the WOS Advanced Governance Specification v1.0 (Layer 3). Targets a WOS Kernel Document and declares verifiable constraint subsets (SMT), equity guardrails, constraint zones (DCR-style), multi-step sessions, tool use governance, agent lifecycle state machines, calibration methods, drift detection methods, shadow mode, and circuit breaker patterns. These capabilities serve any complex workflow -- DCR constraint zones model compliance rules for human case management, equity monitoring applies to human decisions. WOS MUST NOT alter core Formspec processing semantics.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosAdvancedGovernance.description: WOS Advanced Governance specification version. MUST be '1.0'. Identifies this document as a WOS Advanced Governance Document (Layer 3) and pins the specification version. Processors reject documents with unsupported versions.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker and version pin for WOS Advanced Governance documents

targetWorkflow.description: URI of the WOS Kernel Document this advanced governance document targets. The Layer 3 processor applies equity guardrails, constraint zones, agent lifecycle rules, and verifiable constraints from this document to the identified workflow.

x-lm.intent: Binds this advanced governance document to a specific kernel workflow

version.description: Version of this Advanced Governance Document. SemVer is RECOMMENDED. Increment when equity guardrails, constraint zones, or lifecycle configurations change.

title.description: Human-readable name for this advanced governance configuration. Displayed in tooling and governance reports.

description.description: Human-readable description of this advanced governance document's scope and purpose. Documents which advanced capabilities are configured and why.
examples: Adds equity monitoring, DCR constraint zones, SMT verifiable constraints, and circuit breaker to the benefits adjudication workflow.

equityGuardrails.description: Equity guardrails monitoring statistical disparities across demographic groupings (Advanced S3). Applies to human AND AI decisions. Evaluated asynchronously.

constraintZones.description: DCR-style constraint zones for adaptive case management (Advanced S4). Activities governed by declarative relations, not explicit transitions.

x-lm.intent: Adaptive case management phases where valid actions are governed by DCR relations

multiStepSessions.description: Multi-step session definitions with checkpoints and intervention points (Advanced S5).

toolGovernance.description: Tool use governance: permitted/prohibited registries, side-effect policy (Advanced S6).

agentLifecycle.description: Agent lifecycle state machine configuration (Advanced S7).

verifiableConstraints.description: Constraints annotated for SMT verification (Advanced S8).

shadowMode.description: Shadow mode configuration (Advanced S11.1). RECOMMENDED starting point for agents in rights-impacting workflows.

circuitBreaker.description: Circuit breaker pattern for automatic fallback on high error rates (Advanced S11.2).

id.description: Unique equity guardrail identifier within this advanced governance document. Referenced in violation provenance records and equity reports.

metric.description: The workflow outcome metric monitored for statistical disparity across demographic groups. Must correspond to a measurable outcome available in workflow provenance records. Applies to both human and AI decisions.

x-lm.intent: The workflow outcome being monitored for statistical disparity

groupBy.description: FEL path into the case file that identifies the demographic or categorical grouping dimension. The equity engine partitions outcomes by the values found at this path across all cases in the evaluation window.

x-lm.intent: The demographic or categorical dimension across which disparity is measured

maxDisparity.description: Maximum acceptable difference (0.0–1.0) between the highest and lowest group metric rates. Expressed as an absolute proportion. Exceeding this threshold triggers the onViolation action.

evaluationWindow.description: ISO 8601 duration over which cases are aggregated for disparity evaluation. Longer windows reduce noise but delay detection of emerging disparities.

minimumSampleSize.description: Minimum number of observations required per group before disparity is considered statistically meaningful. Groups below this threshold are excluded from disparity calculations.

onViolation.description: Action taken when disparity exceeds maxDisparity. 'flag': record in provenance only. 'alert': notify configured notifyRoles. 'suspend': alert plus suspend further autonomous processing until reviewed.

items.description: A transition tag identifying the workflow decision events whose outcomes are included in this guardrail's metric calculation.

monitorTags.description: Transition tags whose outcomes are monitored.

items.description: A WOS role identifier whose members are notified when this equity guardrail fires.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why this guardrail exists and what equity concern it addresses. Documents the regulatory basis, policy rationale, or civil rights obligation being monitored.
examples: Monitors for geographic disparity in benefits approval rates per agency equity plan requirement §3.2.

id.description: Unique constraint zone identifier within this advanced governance document. Referenced in transition configurations to activate and deactivate this adaptive case management zone.

description.description: Human-readable description of this constraint zone's purpose, activation conditions, and the adaptive case management phase it models.
examples: Fraud investigation zone activated when fraud indicators are detected. Activities are governed by DCR relations requiring interviews before the final report.

activities.description: Activities within this constraint zone with initial markings.

x-lm.intent: The set of activities available in this adaptive case management phase
Declarative constraints governing which activities can execute and when

completionEvent.description: Event raised when all pending activities in this constraint zone are completed and no obligations remain. Triggers transition to the next workflow phase.

id.description: Activity identifier within this constraint zone. Referenced by DCR relations as source or target. Must be unique within the zone's activities list.

activityRef.description: Reference URI to the activity definition that describes this activity's preconditions, actor requirements, and expected outputs. Used for documentation and tooling integration.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this activity involves and when it should be performed in the case investigation.
examples: Interview a witness or applicant about suspected fraud indicators to gather sworn testimony.

initialIncluded.description: Whether this activity starts as included (available for execution) when the zone is activated. DCR include/exclude relations may change this marking during zone execution.

initialPending.description: Whether this activity starts as pending (must be executed at least once before the zone can complete). DCR condition and response relations may also add pending obligations.

type.description: DCR relation type governing the constraint between source and target activities (Advanced S4.3). 'condition': target requires source executed first. 'response': executing source creates a pending obligation on target. 'include': executing source includes target. 'exclude': executing source excludes target. 'milestone': target can only execute while source is pending.

x-lm.intent: Determines the constraint semantics between two activities

source.description: Identifier of the source activity in this DCR relation. Must match an activity id declared in the enclosing constraint zone's activities list.

target.description: Identifier of the target activity in this DCR relation. Must match an activity id declared in the enclosing constraint zone's activities list.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of why this DCR relation exists and what compliance rule or workflow constraint it encodes.
examples: Cannot write the final report until at least one witness interview has been conducted.

id.description: Unique multi-step session identifier within this advanced governance document. Referenced in agent task configurations to associate a task with this session definition.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this multi-step session accomplishes and which workflow task or agent operation it governs.
examples: Multi-step session for analyzing an evidence package: inventories documents, extracts facts, cross-references against case data, and produces findings.

maxSteps.description: Maximum number of steps the agent may execute in this session before it is forcibly terminated. Guards against infinite loops and excessive token consumption.

maxDuration.description: Maximum wall-clock duration for the entire session. ISO 8601 duration. The processor MUST terminate and record a timeout if exceeded.

checkpointPolicy.description: When to persist session state to durable storage. 'afterEachStep': after every step completes. 'atInterventionPoints': only at steps marked as intervention points. 'onDemand': only when explicitly requested.

id.description: Unique step identifier within this session. Referenced by other steps' dependsOn lists and by audit/provenance records.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this step accomplishes and what outputs it produces for subsequent steps.
examples: Catalog all documents in the evidence package and classify by type and relevance.

items.description: Identifier of a step that must complete successfully before this step can begin execution.

dependsOn.description: Step identifiers that must complete before this step.

interventionPoint.description: Whether this step is a human intervention point where execution pauses for review. When true, the processor suspends the session and records an intervention request in the task list.

interventionPrompt.description: Human-readable prompt displayed to the reviewer at this intervention point. Describes what intermediate results are available and what the reviewer should assess before allowing the session to continue.
examples: Review the agent's document inventory and extracted facts before it produces the final recommendation.

defaultSideEffectPolicy.description: Default policy applied to tools with side effects when the agent is operating at autonomous autonomy level. 'block': prevent execution. 'allow': permit execution. 'requireApproval': pause and request human approval.

id.description: Unique tool identifier within the tool governance registry. Referenced in agent invocation logs and side-effect policy enforcement.

category.description: Functional category of this tool. Used to apply category-level governance policies. Standard categories: 'dataRetrieval', 'computation', 'externalApi'. Custom categories are permitted.

sideEffects.description: Whether this tool has external side effects (writes, sends, modifies external state). Tools with side effects are subject to the defaultSideEffectPolicy when the agent is at autonomous autonomy level.

outputSchema.description: Expected JSON Schema for this tool's output. Used by validation tooling to verify that tool outputs conform to declared contracts before they are mapped to case state.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this tool does, which external systems it connects to, and any data handling or privacy implications.
examples: Queries the internal case management database for prior applications by SSN.

maxPerMinute.description: Maximum number of times this tool may be invoked per minute across all agent sessions. When exceeded, the tool call is blocked and recorded as a rate-limit violation in provenance.

maxPerHour.description: Maximum number of times this tool may be invoked per hour across all agent sessions. Enforced independently of maxPerMinute; both limits must not be exceeded.

initialState.description: Initial lifecycle state for agents entering this workflow. 'active': full operation at configured autonomy level. 'degraded': reduced autonomy capped at degradedAutonomyCap. 'suspended': all agent participation blocked pending review.

transitions.description: Lifecycle transition triggers. When omitted, transitions are manual-only.

degradedAutonomyCap.description: Maximum autonomy level permitted when an agent is in the degraded lifecycle state. Overrides the task-level autonomy configuration. Default 'assistive' ensures human review of all outputs during degraded operation.

versionChangeNotification.description: Whether to notify suspensionNotifyRoles when the underlying model version changes. Enables governance teams to reassess calibration and performance after model updates.

items.description: A WOS role identifier whose members are notified when an agent transitions to the suspended lifecycle state.

suspensionNotifyRoles.description: Roles to notify when an agent is suspended.

name.description: Lifecycle transition name (Advanced S7.2). 'demote': active → degraded. 'restore': degraded → active. 'suspend': any → suspended. 'resume': suspended → previous state. 'retire': any → retired (terminal).

trigger.description: Condition expression that automatically triggers this lifecycle transition. May be a FEL expression evaluated against agent metrics or a human-readable description for documentation purposes. When omitted, the transition is manual-only.

description.description: Human-readable description of when and why this lifecycle transition fires. Documents the monitoring rationale and expected administrative response.
examples: Sustained low confidence over 7 days triggers demotion to degraded state.

constraintRef.description: Reference to a deontic constraint id declared in the AI Integration Document. This annotation marks it for SMT formal verification using the AdvGov S8.2 verifiable subset. The verifier checks all expressions referencing this constraint.

x-lm.intent: Identifies which deontic constraint is subject to SMT verification

verifiable.description: MUST be true. Annotates this constraint entry for SMT verification. The value 'true' is the only valid value and is required to distinguish annotation entries from documentation-only entries.

expectedResult.description: Expected verification result from prior verification runs. Used by CI tooling to detect regressions when the constraint is re-verified after code or policy changes.

notes.description: Notes on verifiability explaining which SMT subset restrictions apply, why a result may be inconclusive, or what manual review is needed to complement formal verification.
examples: Uses only linear arithmetic and finite domain equality checks. Fully within the verifiable subset.

expression.description: FEL expression checked by verification tooling against the AdvGov S8.2 SMT verifiable subset. Optional when the constraint is documented only in notes. The verifier parses, linearizes, and submits this expression to the configured SMT solver.

finiteDomainDeclarations.description: Optional map from dotted field paths (as used in FEL, e.g. `output.status`) to explicit finite value domains. Suppresses variable-to-variable equality warnings in SMT subset linting when at least one side of the equality is a listed path.

items.description: A single possible value in the finite domain for this field path. All values must be string-typed and collectively exhaustive for the path's expected range.

domain.description: Closed set of string values for the path.

method.description: Calibration method for aligning agent confidence scores with empirical accuracy (Advanced S9.2). 'plattScaling': logistic regression on scores. 'isotonic': non-parametric monotone regression. 'binning': histogram-based calibration. 'custom': implementation-defined.

minimumSamples.description: Minimum number of reviewed agent outputs required before a calibration run is considered statistically valid. Runs with fewer samples are skipped.

retrainingFrequency.description: How often to recalibrate confidence scores against accumulated reviewed outputs. ISO 8601 duration. Shorter intervals catch drift faster but require more frequent review accumulation.

method.description: Statistical method for detecting drift in agent output distributions (Advanced S10.2). 'psi': Population Stability Index. 'ks': Kolmogorov-Smirnov test. 'chi2': chi-squared test for categorical data. 'accuracy': track empirical accuracy against human decisions.

threshold.description: Threshold value at which drift is considered significant and triggers the configured drift response. The interpretation depends on the method: PSI > 0.2 is significant; KS p-value < 0.05; accuracy drop > 0.10.

window.description: Evaluation window over which drift statistics are computed. ISO 8601 duration. Shorter windows detect rapid drift; longer windows smooth noise.

field.description: FEL path or field name in the agent's output whose distribution is monitored for drift. Must match a field produced by the agent in its case state output.

type.description: Statistical type of this drift dimension. 'continuous': numeric values (use PSI or KS). 'categorical': enumerated values (use chi2 or PSI on proportions).

enabled.description: Whether shadow mode is active for agents in this workflow. When true, agent outputs are generated but not applied to case state; they are compared against the compareTo source to evaluate readiness.

compareTo.description: The baseline to compare shadow agent outputs against. 'human': compare against decisions made by human caseworkers. 'productionAgent': compare against an already-deployed agent version.

duration.description: How long the agent operates in shadow mode before evaluation. ISO 8601 duration. After this period, tooling generates a readiness report comparing shadow outputs to the baseline.

minimumSamples.description: Minimum number of shadow cases required before the readiness evaluation is considered statistically valid. Shadow mode continues until both duration and minimumSamples are satisfied.

enabled.description: Whether the circuit breaker is active for agents in this workflow. When true, the processor monitors error rates and automatically suspends agent participation when the threshold is exceeded.

errorRateThreshold.description: Error rate proportion (0.0–1.0) that trips the circuit breaker and suspends agent participation. Measured over the evaluationWindow after minimumInvocations is reached.

evaluationWindow.description: Rolling time window over which the error rate is calculated. ISO 8601 duration. Shorter windows detect errors faster but are more sensitive to transient spikes.

minimumInvocations.description: Minimum number of agent invocations within the evaluationWindow before the circuit breaker can trip. Prevents false positives from small sample sizes early in deployment.

cooldownDuration.description: How long the circuit breaker remains open (agent suspended) before allowing probe invocations to test recovery. ISO 8601 duration.

probeCount.description: Number of successful probe invocations required to close the circuit breaker and restore normal agent participation after a cooldown period.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
